checks.name: schema_completeness
checks.detail: All required payload fields present and substantive

checks.name: flow_integrity
checks.detail: 3 flows: flow_webhook_processing (17 nodes/21 edges), flow_weekly_report (13 nodes/14 edges), flow_periodic_spike_check (11 nodes/12 edges). All well-connected with error nodes.

checks.name: trigger_validity
checks.detail: 3 triggers: webhook (sendgrid events), schedule (0 9 * * 1 weekly), schedule (*/15 * * * * every 15 min). All valid.

checks.name: connector_role_alignment
checks.detail: sendgrid=email_delivery, airtable=knowledge_base, slack=chat_messaging — all valid roles

checks.name: adoption_question_fitness
checks.detail: 5 adoption_questions added: alert_channel, critical_channel, airtable_base_id, bounce_spike_threshold, auto_suppress_engaged

checks.name: safety_scan
checks.detail: 0 critical findings. PII hashing mentioned. Compliance safety (never un-suppress without approval) explicitly handled.

dimensions.name: Prompt Completeness
dimensions.rationale: Identity is 70+ words. Instructions cover 6 major sections with detailed logic (webhook ingestion, Airtable dashboard, spike detection, alerting, auto-suppression, weekly report). Tool guidance has specific API endpoints for all 3 services. Examples have 3 concrete scenarios. Error handling covers all services plus compliance.

dimensions.name: Tool-Prompt Alignment
dimensions.rationale: http_request, file_read, file_write all mapped to specific API calls. SendGrid, Airtable, Slack APIs all documented with endpoints and body formats. No phantom tools.

dimensions.name: Trigger Coherence
dimensions.rationale: Webhook for real-time events, Monday 9AM for weekly report, every-15-min for spike detection — all three match prompt workflow descriptions precisely.

dimensions.name: Connector Coverage
dimensions.rationale: All 3 service_flow entries (SendGrid, Airtable, Slack) backed by connectors with complete credential fields.

dimensions.name: Memory Design
dimensions.rationale: Rolling baselines in deliverability_baselines.json, deduplication via last_processed_event.json, agent_memory for calibrated thresholds. Good scope.

dimensions.name: Error Handling
dimensions.rationale: Comprehensive: SendGrid retry with backoff, Airtable rate limits (5 req/s) with batching, Slack fallback to admin DM, duplicate webhook deduplication, stale baseline recovery, PII protection, compliance safety rules.

dimensions.name: Use Case Fidelity
dimensions.rationale: All 3 use_case_flows fully described in prompt: webhook processing with suppression logic, weekly report with trend analysis, periodic spike detection with baseline comparison.

dimensions.name: Value Clarity
dimensions.rationale: Clear business outcome: protect sender reputation, maintain deliverability dashboard, automated suppression with compliance. Replaces 5 separate workflows.

dimensions.name: Execution Feasibility
dimensions.rationale: SendGrid, Airtable, Slack APIs are standard. Webhook setup requires SendGrid configuration. All trigger configs are actionable. Four-table Airtable setup is substantial but well-documented.

dimensions.name: Differentiation
dimensions.rationale: Significant reasoning: bounce classification, spike detection with rolling baselines, safety checks for recently-engaged addresses, compliance decisions. Well beyond cron capabilities.

dimensions.name: Variable Necessity
dimensions.rationale: All 5 questions target genuine config needs. bounce_spike_threshold and auto_suppress_engaged add meaningful customization beyond just connector config.

dimensions.name: Default Quality
dimensions.rationale: Good defaults for channels and threshold. Airtable base_id must be user-provided. Overall, user needs to provide 1 value (base_id) minimum to get started.

issues: No adoption_questions existed — 5 added covering channel routing, Airtable config, thresholds, and suppression policy
SendGrid and Airtable connectors may not have builtin definitions

template: Added 5 adoption_questions: alert_channel, critical_channel, airtable_base_id, bounce_spike_threshold, auto_suppress_engaged
Included use_case_ids on threshold and suppression questions to scope them to relevant flows
